project.name: Allegro WebSocket Swing PoC
project.description: Proof-of-concept for Allegro system modernization. A Vue.js search UI finds customers from a mock dataset and transfers selected records via WebSocket relay to a Java Swing desktop form, simulating legacy Allegro data entry automation.

components.name: websocket.Main (Legacy Swing Client)
responsibilities: Connect to ws://localhost:1337/ via Tyrus WebSocketContainer
Receive JSON messages and route by 'target' field
Populate 10 form fields from deserialized SearchResult
Maintain CountDownLatch to keep main thread alive

components.name: PocPresenter
responsibilities: Bind all 13 JTextComponent/JRadioButton fields to ModelProperties via DocumentListener/ChangeListener
EventEmitter subscription: on event, update textArea and clear all form fields

components.name: PocModel
methods: action(): collects all fields, POSTs to HttpBinService, emits response via EventEmitter

components.name: HttpBinService
components.description: Serializes Map<String,String> of all form fields to JSON, sends HTTP POST, returns response body. Backed by httpbin Docker container (kennethreitz/httpbin).

components.name: ValueModel<T>
components.description: Generic wrapper for a single typed field with get/set. Enables type-safe storage in EnumMap.

components.name: WebsocketServer.js
components.description: HTTP+WebSocket server. Maintains clients[] array. On UTF8 message: broadcasts verbatim to all connected clients. On close: splices client from array.

components.name: App.vue
components.description: Root layout: red header 'Search Mock' (#ff2b06), renders <Search> child component.

components.name: Search.vue
components.description: Core search UI. Connects to ws://localhost:1337 on mount. Provides multi-field search over in-memory mock dataset (5 customers). Sends selected customer + chosen Zahlungsempfänger via WebSocket.

data_flow.primary_flow: Vue Search → WebSocket (ws://localhost:1337) → Node.js relay → Java Swing Client → UI fields populated
data_flow.secondary_flow: Java Swing UI → 'Anordnen' button → PocModel.action() → HTTP POST to httpbin:8080 → Response emitted → View updated

ratings.architecture: 3/5 — MVP pattern in com/poc is clean; websocket/Main.java is monolithic
ratings.separation_of_concerns: 3/5 — Good in MVP path; poor in legacy websocket/Main.java
ratings.error_handling: 2/5 — Exceptions wrapped in RuntimeException; no user-facing error dialogs
ratings.json_parsing: 2/5 — Manual flag-based streaming parser is fragile; no POJO deserialization
ratings.security: 2/5 — No CORS/origin validation in WS server; no input sanitization; hardcoded localhost URLs
ratings.code_duplication: WARNING — websocket/Main.java and PocView.java duplicate the entire Swing UI layout
ratings.scalability: 2/5 — In-memory mock data; no persistence layer

critical_issues: Duplicate UI Code — websocket/Main.java and PocView.java implement identical Swing layouts
Manual JSON Parsing — Fragile flag-based streaming parser instead of object mapping
No WebSocket Integration in MVP Path — com.Main / PocPresenter has no WebSocket code
Static Mock Data — search_space is hardcoded in Search.vue (no real backend search)
Error handling swallowed — RuntimeException wrapping hides failures silently
